items.title: Repository has no commits (not a git repo or empty)
items.rationale: Repository has no commits (not a git repo or empty).
items.fix_hint:
If you imported the project as a single squash, re-import preserving
history (`git filter-repo` or a fresh clone of the source). For new
projects, just keep committing — agents need the trail.

items.title: Gitignore: Covers Junk
items.rationale: .gitignore covers 6/12 groups; missing: python_pycache, go_vendor, rust_target, jvm_class, python_egg_info, terraform.
items.fix_hint:
Run `gitignore.io` for your stack and paste the result into
`.gitignore`. At minimum, cover: `__pycache__`, `node_modules`,
`.env`, `dist/`, `build/`, `*.log`, IDE settings, coverage dirs.

items.title: None of these expected paths exist:
items.rationale: None of these expected paths exist: .gitleaks.toml, .gitleaks.yaml, .gitleaks.yml, gitleaks.toml, .trufflehog.yaml, .trufflehog.yml, .secrets.baseline, .pre-commit-config.yaml, .github/workflows/gitleaks.yml, .github/workflows/secret-scan.yml
items.fix_hint:
Add a `.gitleaks.toml` (or `.secrets.baseline` for detect-secrets) and
wire it into pre-commit or a GitHub Actions workflow. Example:
https://github.com/gitleaks/gitleaks-action.

items.title: README is missing signals for: test
items.rationale: README is missing signals for: test.
items.fix_hint:
Add three short sections to README: Install (one fenced block),
Run (one fenced block showing the canonical entry point), Test
(one fenced block). Three fenced blocks is the floor; if your
project genuinely needs more, write more.

items.title: At least one reproducible-workspace config is present
items.rationale: At least one reproducible-workspace config is present. (no clause matched)
items.fix_hint:
Drop a minimal `.devcontainer/devcontainer.json` referencing a
Microsoft base image plus `postCreateCommand: pip install -e .`.
For Nix shops, `flake.nix` with a dev shell is the equivalent.
